articles.headline: Government Unveils Comprehensive National Digital Infrastructure Act 2025
articles.explainer:
• The Union Cabinet approved the draft National Digital Infrastructure Act (NDIA) 2025, aiming to consolidate existing laws on data governance, cybersecurity, and digital public infrastructure.
• The Act proposes a unified regulatory framework for digital services, focusing on data privacy, secure digital transactions, and responsible AI development.
• Key provisions include mandatory data localization for critical sectors, establishment of a National Digital Authority (NDA) for oversight, and penalties for data breaches.
• It seeks to balance innovation with user protection, addressing concerns around digital monopolies and ensuring equitable access to digital services.
• NDIA 2025 is expected to replace fragmented laws and provide a robust legal foundation for India's growing digital economy.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors
Information and Communication Technologies
Cybersecurity
Data Protection
E-governance
prelimsPoints: NDIA 2025 aims to consolidate laws on data governance, cybersecurity, and DPI.
It proposes the establishment of a National Digital Authority (NDA).
Key aspects include data localization and penalties for data breaches.
The Act seeks to replace fragmented existing digital laws.
mainsPoints: Discuss the necessity of a unified digital infrastructure act in the context of India's digital growth and evolving cyber threats.
Analyze the potential impact of data localization mandates on foreign tech companies and India's digital economy.
Evaluate the role of the proposed National Digital Authority (NDA) in ensuring regulatory coherence and effective enforcement.
Critically examine how the Act balances innovation, user protection, and national security concerns in the digital space.
Elaborate on the challenges and opportunities in implementing a comprehensive digital governance framework like NDIA 2025.
articles.imageDescription: Illustration of digital infrastructure with data streams and security symbols.
articles.trivia: India is one of the fastest-growing digital economies, with over 800 million internet users as of early 2020s.

articles.headline: RBI Maintains Status Quo on Repo Rate Amidst Inflationary Concerns
articles.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) concluded its bi-monthly review, opting to keep the repo rate unchanged at 6.5%.
• The decision reflects a cautious approach, balancing persistent inflationary pressures with the need to support economic growth.
• RBI Governor highlighted global uncertainties and domestic food inflation as key factors influencing the MPC's stance.
• The central bank reiterated its commitment to gradually withdrawing accommodation to ensure inflation aligns with the target band.
• Analysts suggest that future rate cuts would be contingent on a sustained decline in core inflation and stable global economic conditions.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning
mobilization of resources
growth
development and employment
Monetary Policy
prelimsPoints: RBI's MPC kept the repo rate unchanged at 6.5%.
The decision was driven by inflationary concerns and global uncertainties.
RBI aims to bring inflation within its target band.
Future rate cuts depend on sustained decline in core inflation.
mainsPoints: Analyze the factors influencing RBI's decision to maintain the status quo on the repo rate.
Discuss the challenges faced by the MPC in balancing inflation control and economic growth objectives.
Evaluate the effectiveness of monetary policy tools in addressing supply-side inflationary pressures, particularly food inflation.
Examine the potential implications of the current monetary stance on various sectors of the Indian economy, including lending and investment.
Critically assess the role of global economic conditions and geopolitical events in shaping India's domestic monetary policy.
articles.imageDescription: RBI headquarters building with financial graphs overlaid.
articles.trivia: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) in India consists of six members – three officials from the RBI and three external members nominated by the Government of India.

articles.headline: India and France Deepen Strategic Partnership in Annual Dialogue
articles.explainer:
• The annual India-France Strategic Dialogue concluded in New Delhi, focusing on enhanced cooperation in defence, space, and emerging technologies.
• Both nations reaffirmed their commitment to a free, open, and inclusive Indo-Pacific, agreeing to coordinate efforts on maritime security and regional stability.
• Discussions included joint defence manufacturing initiatives, transfer of critical technologies, and collaboration on sustainable development goals.
• India's National Security Advisor and the French President's Diplomatic Advisor led the talks, underscoring the high-level engagement.
• The dialogue is a cornerstone of the India-France strategic partnership, which has gained momentum in recent years.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests
prelimsPoints: Annual India-France Strategic Dialogue held in New Delhi.
Focus areas include defence, space, and emerging technologies.
Both nations reiterated commitment to a free Indo-Pacific.
Discussions covered joint manufacturing and technology transfer.
mainsPoints: Analyze the significance of the India-France strategic partnership in the evolving geopolitical landscape of the Indo-Pacific.
Discuss the role of defence and space cooperation in strengthening India's strategic autonomy and technological capabilities.
Evaluate how India and France can collaborate to address global challenges such as climate change and sustainable development.
Examine the implications of enhanced bilateral ties on India's 'Make in India' initiative, particularly in the defence sector.
Critically assess the convergences and divergences in India and France's approach to multilateralism and global governance.
articles.imageDescription: Flags of India and France waving together.
articles.trivia: France was the first P5 nation to establish a strategic partnership with India in 1998.

articles.headline: Ministry of Environment Launches 'Project Dolphin' Expansion Phase
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change announced the expansion of 'Project Dolphin', focusing on enhanced protection for Gangetic River Dolphins.
• The new phase includes increased surveillance, community engagement programs, and habitat restoration efforts along major river basins.
• Emphasis is placed on reducing anthropogenic pressures, such as pollution and illegal fishing, which threaten the species.
• Collaboration with local communities and state governments is a key component to ensure sustainable conservation practices.
• Project Dolphin, initiated in 2020, aims to conserve both riverine and oceanic dolphins in India.
gsSubTopics: Conservation
environmental pollution and degradation
environmental impact assessment
Biodiversity
prelimsPoints: Ministry of Environment launched expansion of 'Project Dolphin'.
Focus on Gangetic River Dolphins, India's National Aquatic Animal.
Key strategies include surveillance, community engagement, habitat restoration.
Aims to reduce pollution and illegal fishing.
Project Dolphin was initiated in 2020.
mainsPoints: Discuss the ecological significance of the Gangetic River Dolphin and its role as an indicator species for river health.
Analyze the major threats to the survival of Gangetic River Dolphins and the effectiveness of current conservation strategies.
Evaluate the importance of community participation and inter-state cooperation in the success of conservation projects like 'Project Dolphin'.
Examine the policy challenges in balancing developmental activities with biodiversity conservation in riverine ecosystems.
Suggest innovative approaches for protecting endangered aquatic species in India, drawing lessons from 'Project Dolphin' and 'Project Tiger'.
articles.imageDescription: A Gangetic River Dolphin breaching the water surface.
articles.trivia: The Gangetic River Dolphin (Platanista gangetica) is one of the four freshwater dolphin species in the world and is listed as 'Endangered' by the IUCN.

articles.headline: ISRO Provides Update on Lunar Polar Exploration Mission (LUPEX) Preparations
articles.explainer:
• ISRO announced significant progress in the preparations for the Lunar Polar Exploration Mission (LUPEX), a joint venture with JAXA (Japan Aerospace Exploration Agency).
• The mission aims to explore the Moon's polar regions, particularly for water-ice and potential resources, using a rover and a lander.
• Indian scientists are currently integrating advanced scientific payloads and conducting rigorous testing of the rover's mobility systems.
• LUPEX is expected to launch in the latter half of 2026, marking another milestone in India's lunar exploration program.
• The collaboration highlights growing international partnerships in space exploration and shared scientific objectives.
gsSubTopics: Achievements of Indians in science & technology
indigenization of technology and developing new technology
Space technology
prelimsPoints: LUPEX is a joint mission between ISRO and JAXA.
It aims to explore the Moon's polar regions for water-ice.
The mission involves a rover and a lander.
Expected launch in late 2026.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of lunar polar regions for future space exploration and resource utilization.
Analyze the benefits and challenges of international collaborations like LUPEX in advancing space science and technology.
Evaluate India's growing capabilities in lunar exploration, drawing parallels with previous Chandrayaan missions.
Examine the potential scientific outcomes of the LUPEX mission, particularly regarding the presence and distribution of water-ice on the Moon.
Critically assess the role of space technology in fostering national prestige and contributing to technological self-reliance.
articles.imageDescription: Artistic rendering of a lunar rover exploring the Moon's surface.
articles.trivia: India's Chandrayaan-1 mission in 2008 famously confirmed the presence of water molecules on the lunar surface.

articles.headline: National Logistics Policy Shows Promising Progress, Targets Efficiency Boost
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Commerce and Industry released a progress report on the National Logistics Policy (NLP), highlighting key achievements since its launch.
• The report noted a significant improvement in India's Logistics Performance Index (LPI) ranking and a reduction in logistics costs as a percentage of GDP.
• Initiatives like the Unified Logistics Interface Platform (ULIP) and the National Logistics Portal (NLP) are streamlining operations and enhancing transparency.
• Infrastructure development, including multi-modal parks and dedicated freight corridors, has played a crucial role in improving connectivity.
• The government aims to further reduce logistics costs to global benchmarks, enhancing India's competitiveness in manufacturing and trade.
gsSubTopics: Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Investment models
Indian Economy and issues relating to planning
prelimsPoints: National Logistics Policy (NLP) progress report released by Ministry of Commerce.
Report indicates improvement in India's Logistics Performance Index (LPI).
ULIP and National Logistics Portal are key initiatives.
Infrastructure development like multi-modal parks are crucial.
Aim is to reduce logistics costs to global benchmarks.
mainsPoints: Discuss the objectives and key pillars of the National Logistics Policy and its role in transforming India's supply chain ecosystem.
Analyze the impact of reduced logistics costs on India's manufacturing sector, exports, and overall economic competitiveness.
Evaluate the effectiveness of digital platforms like ULIP in enhancing efficiency, transparency, and integration across the logistics sector.
Examine the challenges in infrastructure development and last-mile connectivity that still need to be addressed for the full realization of NLP's goals.
Critically assess how the NLP aligns with broader government initiatives such as 'Make in India' and 'PM Gati Shakti'.
articles.imageDescription: A graphic showing interconnected logistics networks and transport modes.
articles.trivia: India aims to bring down logistics costs from the current 13-14% of GDP to global benchmarks of 8%.

articles.headline: Supreme Court Bench to Review Electoral Bonds Transparency Petitions
articles.explainer:
• A special bench of the Supreme Court of India is set to hear a series of petitions challenging the transparency and constitutionality of the Electoral Bonds scheme.
• Petitioners argue that the scheme promotes anonymous funding to political parties, undermining electoral transparency and the right to information.
• The government has defended the scheme as a measure to curb black money in political funding, ensuring donor anonymity.
• The court's decision is anticipated to have significant implications for electoral funding reforms and political accountability in India.
• Previous hearings have focused on the balance between donor privacy and public's right to know the source of political funds.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure
Separation of powers between various organs dispute redressal mechanisms and institutions
Salient features of the Representation of People’s Act
prelimsPoints: Supreme Court bench to review petitions on Electoral Bonds scheme.
Petitions challenge transparency and constitutionality of the scheme.
Government defends the scheme as a curb on black money.
Scheme allows anonymous funding to political parties.
mainsPoints: Discuss the constitutional validity and ethical concerns surrounding the Electoral Bonds scheme in India.
Analyze how the Electoral Bonds scheme impacts electoral transparency and the public's right to know about political funding.
Evaluate the arguments for and against donor anonymity in political financing, considering its implications for democratic processes.
Examine the role of the judiciary in ensuring electoral reforms and upholding the principles of free and fair elections.
Suggest alternative mechanisms for political funding that can enhance transparency while addressing the issue of black money in elections.
articles.imageDescription: Supreme Court of India building facade.
articles.trivia: Electoral bonds were introduced in India through the Finance Act, 2017, and later amendments to the Reserve Bank of India Act, 1934, and the Representation of the People Act, 1951.

articles.headline: Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission Crosses Major Milestones in Digital Health Adoption
articles.explainer:
• The National Health Authority (NHA) announced that the Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM) has achieved significant milestones, including over 500 million Ayushman Bharat Health Accounts (ABHA).
• The mission is rapidly expanding its network of healthcare providers and digital health records, enhancing accessibility and efficiency.
• Integration of private hospitals and clinics into the ABDM ecosystem is progressing, facilitating seamless sharing of patient data with consent.
• The digital health ecosystem aims to empower individuals with control over their health records and improve healthcare delivery across the nation.
• Future plans include leveraging AI and machine learning for predictive analytics in public health.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources
E-governance- applications, models, successes, limitations, and potential
prelimsPoints: ABDM achieved over 500 million Ayushman Bharat Health Accounts (ABHA).
NHA is the implementing agency for ABDM.
Mission aims to create a digital health ecosystem.
Focus on integrating private healthcare providers.
Future plans include AI for predictive analytics in health.
mainsPoints: Discuss the transformative potential of the Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission in revolutionizing healthcare delivery and access in India.
Analyze the challenges involved in ensuring data privacy and security within a vast digital health ecosystem like ABDM.
Evaluate the role of ABHA in empowering individuals and promoting patient-centric healthcare.
Examine how ABDM can bridge the healthcare divide between urban and rural areas and improve health outcomes.
Critically assess the implications of integrating AI and machine learning into public health initiatives under ABDM for disease surveillance and prevention.
articles.imageDescription: A graphic depicting interconnected digital health records and patient profiles.
articles.trivia: The Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission was launched nationwide in September 2021, building on a pilot project.

articles.headline: India Prepares for Crucial Role in Upcoming UN Climate Summit (COP30)
articles.explainer:
• India's Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change outlined its priorities and negotiation strategies for the upcoming UN Climate Summit (COP30 in Brazil).
• India will advocate for equitable climate action, emphasizing the principle of Common but Differentiated Responsibilities and Respective Capabilities (CBDR-RC).
• Focus areas include securing enhanced climate finance for developing nations, technology transfer for green solutions, and adaptation measures.
• India plans to highlight its domestic achievements in renewable energy expansion and climate resilience, urging developed nations to meet their commitments.
• The summit is expected to finalize mechanisms for global carbon markets and review progress on the Paris Agreement goals.
gsSubTopics: Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests
Environmental pollution and degradation
keyTerms: Common but Differentiated Responsibilities and Respective Capabilities (CBDR-RC)
prelimsPoints: India outlined priorities for COP30 in Brazil.
Advocating for CBDR-RC principle.
Key demands: enhanced climate finance and technology transfer.
India will highlight its renewable energy achievements.
COP30 to finalize global carbon market mechanisms.
mainsPoints: Discuss India's historical stance on climate change negotiations and its evolving role in global climate diplomacy.
Analyze the significance of the CBDR-RC principle for developing nations like India in achieving climate justice.
Evaluate the challenges in securing adequate climate finance and technology transfer from developed to developing countries.
Examine India's domestic climate action initiatives, particularly in renewable energy, and their contribution to its international commitments.
Critically assess the potential outcomes of COP30 regarding global carbon markets and the review of Paris Agreement goals.
articles.imageDescription: Globe with climate change symbols and national flags.
articles.trivia: The first Conference of Parties (COP) to the UNFCCC was held in Berlin, Germany, in 1995.

articles.headline: Government Accelerates Border Infrastructure Development in Northeast India
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Defence announced accelerated projects for border infrastructure development in India's northeastern states, particularly along the LAC.
• The focus is on improving connectivity, strategic roads, bridges, and advanced surveillance systems to enhance border security and troop mobility.
• The initiative is part of a broader strategy to strengthen India's defensive capabilities and facilitate economic development in remote border areas.
• Collaboration with the Border Roads Organisation (BRO) and state governments is crucial for timely project completion.
• The move comes amidst ongoing geopolitical complexities and aims to deter external aggression while ensuring regional stability.
gsSubTopics: Security challenges and their management in border areas
Linkages of organized crime with terrorism
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
prelimsPoints: Ministry of Defence accelerates border infrastructure projects in Northeast India.
Focus on LAC for strategic roads, bridges, and surveillance.
Aims to enhance border security and troop mobility.
BRO is a key executing agency.
Part of broader strategy to strengthen defensive capabilities.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of robust border infrastructure for India's national security, particularly in the northeastern region.
Analyze the challenges faced in developing infrastructure in difficult terrain along the LAC and the role of organizations like BRO.
Evaluate how improved border connectivity can contribute to both military preparedness and the economic development of remote border areas.
Examine the geopolitical implications of India's accelerated infrastructure development along its northern and eastern borders.
Critically assess the balance between environmental concerns and strategic imperatives in undertaking large-scale infrastructure projects in ecologically sensitive regions.
articles.imageDescription: A newly constructed strategic road winding through mountainous terrain in the Himalayas.
articles.trivia: The Border Roads Organisation (BRO) is responsible for building and maintaining road networks in India's border areas and friendly neighboring countries.

articles.headline: Skill India Mission 3.0 Launched with Focus on Future Skills and Green Jobs
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Skill Development and Entrepreneurship launched Skill India Mission 3.0, an upgraded version of its flagship skilling program.
• The new phase emphasizes upskilling and reskilling in emerging technologies like AI, IoT, and data science, aligned with industry 4.0 demands.
• A significant focus is placed on promoting 'green jobs' and sustainable livelihoods, reflecting India's commitment to a green economy.
• The mission aims to integrate formal education with vocational training, ensuring greater employability and entrepreneurship opportunities.
• Public-private partnerships and international collaborations are being strengthened to provide world-class skill development infrastructure.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Human Resources
Skill development and employment
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors
prelimsPoints: Skill India Mission 3.0 launched by Ministry of Skill Development.
Focuses on emerging technologies (AI, IoT) and green jobs.
Aims to integrate formal education with vocational training.
Strengthening public-private partnerships and international collaborations.
Targets industry 4.0 demands.
mainsPoints: Discuss the evolution of Skill India Mission and the necessity for its 3.0 iteration in the context of changing global economic and technological landscapes.
Analyze the potential of 'green jobs' in driving sustainable development and creating new employment opportunities in India.
Evaluate the challenges in bridging the skill gap in emerging technologies and ensuring that India's workforce is future-ready.
Examine the role of public-private partnerships and international collaborations in enhancing the quality and reach of skill development programs.
Critically assess how Skill India Mission 3.0 can contribute to India's demographic dividend and foster an entrepreneurial ecosystem.
articles.imageDescription: Young people engaged in skill training using modern technology.
articles.trivia: The National Skill Development Mission, or Skill India Mission, was launched in 2015 to create convergence across sectors and states in terms of skill training activities.

articles.headline: India's Indigenous Defence Production Receives Major Boost with New Policy Initiatives
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Defence announced a series of policy initiatives aimed at further boosting indigenous defence manufacturing and reducing import dependency.
• The new policies include enhanced funding for R&D in critical defence technologies, streamlined procurement processes for domestic firms, and a revised 'Positive Indigenisation List'.
• Emphasis is placed on leveraging private sector capabilities and fostering a robust defence industrial ecosystem through startups and MSMEs.
• The goal is to achieve self-reliance (Aatmanirbhar Bharat) in defence production, positioning India as a global defence manufacturing hub.
• These measures are expected to create significant employment opportunities and contribute to technological sovereignty.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
Achievements of Indians in science & technology
indigenization of technology and developing new technology
Internal security through communication networks, role of media and social networking sites in internal security challenges, basics of cyber security
prelimsPoints: Ministry of Defence announced new policies for indigenous defence manufacturing.
Includes enhanced R&D funding and streamlined procurement for domestic firms.
Revised 'Positive Indigenisation List' released.
Aims for self-reliance in defence (Aatmanirbhar Bharat).
Focus on private sector, startups, and MSMEs.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic imperative for India to achieve self-reliance in defence production and the challenges involved.
Analyze the impact of 'Positive Indigenisation Lists' and streamlined procurement on boosting domestic defence manufacturing capabilities.
Evaluate the role of the private sector, including startups and MSMEs, in fostering a vibrant defence industrial ecosystem in India.
Examine how increased indigenous production can contribute to India's defence exports and its strategic influence globally.
Critically assess the balance between technological indigenization and acquiring cutting-edge foreign technologies for military modernization.
articles.imageDescription: An Indian-made fighter jet on a runway with technicians.
articles.trivia: India is one of the world's largest importers of defence equipment, but recent policy shifts aim to significantly reduce this dependency.

articles.headline: Government Proposes New Policy Framework for AI Ethics and Responsible Development
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) released a consultation paper on a proposed national policy framework for Artificial Intelligence (AI) ethics.
• The framework aims to guide responsible AI development, deployment, and usage, focusing on principles of fairness, transparency, accountability, and privacy.
• It addresses concerns regarding algorithmic bias, data misuse, and the societal impact of AI, including potential job displacement.
• The policy seeks to foster an innovative AI ecosystem while mitigating risks through regulatory sandboxes and ethical guidelines.
• Stakeholder consultations are invited to finalize the framework, which could set precedents for AI governance in a developing economy.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
Awareness in the fields of IT, Space, Computers, robotics, nano-technology, bio-technology and issues relating to intellectual property rights
Cybersecurity
prelimsPoints: MeitY released consultation paper on AI ethics policy framework.
Focus on fairness, transparency, accountability, privacy in AI.
Addresses algorithmic bias, data misuse, job displacement.
Aims to foster innovation while mitigating risks.
Stakeholder consultations invited for finalization.
mainsPoints: Discuss the ethical challenges posed by the rapid advancement and deployment of Artificial Intelligence in various sectors.
Analyze the key principles that should underpin a national policy framework for responsible AI development and usage.
Evaluate the implications of algorithmic bias on social equity and access to services, and how policy can address it.
Examine the balance between fostering AI innovation and implementing robust regulatory mechanisms to mitigate its risks.
Critically assess the role of government, industry, and civil society in shaping an ethical AI ecosystem in India.
articles.imageDescription: Abstract representation of AI brain with ethical guidelines overlaid.
articles.trivia: The term 'Artificial Intelligence' was coined by John McCarthy in 1956 at the Dartmouth Conference.

articles.headline: India Intensifies Conservation Efforts for Ramsar Wetlands, Focus on Ecosystem Services
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change announced intensified conservation and management plans for India's Ramsar sites.
• The new strategy emphasizes the ecological restoration of degraded wetlands, improving their capacity to provide vital ecosystem services.
• Focus areas include water quality improvement, biodiversity protection, and sustainable livelihood generation for local communities.
• The initiative aims to combat pollution, encroachment, and climate change impacts threatening these crucial ecosystems.
• India, with a growing number of Ramsar sites, is committed to fulfilling its obligations under the Ramsar Convention.
gsSubTopics: Conservation
environmental pollution and degradation
environmental impact assessment
Biodiversity
prelimsPoints: Ministry of Environment announced intensified conservation for Ramsar sites.
Strategy emphasizes ecological restoration and ecosystem services.
Focus on water quality, biodiversity, and local livelihoods.
Aims to combat pollution, encroachment, and climate impacts.
India has a growing number of Ramsar sites.
mainsPoints: Discuss the ecological significance of wetlands and the importance of the Ramsar Convention in their global conservation.
Analyze the major threats faced by wetlands in India, including anthropogenic pressures and climate change.
Evaluate the effectiveness of current conservation strategies for Ramsar sites, particularly in terms of ecological restoration and community involvement.
Examine the concept of 'ecosystem services' provided by wetlands and their economic and social value.
Critically assess the challenges in balancing wetland conservation with developmental needs and population pressures in India.
articles.imageDescription: A serene wetland landscape with various migratory birds.
articles.trivia: The Ramsar Convention on Wetlands is an intergovernmental treaty that provides the framework for national action and international cooperation for the conservation and wise use of wetlands and their resources. It was signed in Ramsar, Iran, in 1971.

articles.headline: Government Unveils 'Smart Cities 2.0' Vision for Sustainable Urban Development
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs launched the 'Smart Cities 2.0' initiative, building upon the successes and lessons learned from the first phase.
• The new vision emphasizes a holistic approach to urban planning, integrating climate resilience, digital governance, and citizen participation.
• Focus areas include sustainable mobility, green infrastructure, waste-to-wealth initiatives, and affordable housing solutions.
• The initiative aims to create 'liveable cities' that are economically vibrant, environmentally sustainable, and socially inclusive.
• Advanced technologies, including AI and IoT, will be leveraged for efficient urban management and public service delivery.
gsSubTopics: Urbanization, their problems and their remedies
Salient features of Indian Society
Role of women and women’s organization, population and associated issues, poverty and developmental issues, urbanization, their problems and their remedies
prelimsPoints: Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs launched 'Smart Cities 2.0'.
Emphasizes climate resilience, digital governance, citizen participation.
Focus areas: sustainable mobility, green infrastructure, waste-to-wealth.
Aims to create 'liveable cities'.
Leverages AI and IoT for urban management.
mainsPoints: Discuss the evolution of India's Smart Cities Mission and the necessity for a 'Smart Cities 2.0' vision in addressing contemporary urban challenges.
Analyze the concept of 'liveable cities' and how the new initiative aims to integrate economic vibrancy, environmental sustainability, and social inclusivity.
Evaluate the role of digital governance and advanced technologies (AI, IoT) in enhancing urban management and public service delivery.
Examine the challenges in implementing sustainable urban planning strategies, particularly in rapidly urbanizing Indian cities.
Critically assess how 'Smart Cities 2.0' can contribute to climate resilience and address issues of urban poverty and inequality.
articles.imageDescription: A futuristic city skyline with green spaces and smart infrastructure.
articles.trivia: The Smart Cities Mission was launched by Prime Minister Narendra Modi in 2015, with the objective of promoting cities that provide core infrastructure and give a decent quality of life to its citizens.

articles.headline: Government Boosts New Generation Agri-Tech Startups for Farmers' Income Enhancement
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers' Welfare announced new incentives and funding mechanisms to support agri-tech startups.
• The focus is on innovative solutions that enhance agricultural productivity, improve supply chain efficiency, and boost farmers' income.
• Initiatives include incubation programs, access to venture capital, and simplified regulatory processes for agri-tech innovations.
• Technologies like precision farming, AI-driven crop management, and drone applications are being promoted to modernize the agricultural sector.
• The move aligns with the broader goal of doubling farmers' income and making Indian agriculture more sustainable and resilient.
gsSubTopics: Major crops-cropping patterns in various parts of the country
different types of irrigation and irrigation system storage
transport and marketing of agricultural produce and issues and related constraints
e-technology in the aid of farmers
Issues related to direct and indirect farm subsidies and minimum support prices
prelimsPoints: Ministry of Agriculture announced incentives for agri-tech startups.
Focus on solutions for productivity, supply chain, and farmers' income.
Initiatives include incubation, venture capital, simplified regulations.
Promoting precision farming, AI crop management, drone applications.
Aims to modernize agriculture and double farmers' income.
mainsPoints: Discuss the role of agri-tech startups in addressing contemporary challenges faced by Indian agriculture, such as low productivity and market access.
Analyze how technology-driven innovations can contribute to doubling farmers' income and making agriculture more sustainable.
Evaluate the effectiveness of government incentives and funding mechanisms in fostering an innovative agri-tech ecosystem.
Examine the challenges in adopting new technologies like precision farming and drones among small and marginal farmers in India.
Critically assess the potential of agri-tech to transform agricultural supply chains and improve post-harvest management.
articles.imageDescription: A drone flying over a farm field, symbolizing precision agriculture.
articles.trivia: India is the second-largest producer of agricultural products worldwide, and agriculture is a significant contributor to its GDP.

articles.headline: India-Pakistan Hold Bilateral Talks on Indus Water Treaty Implementation
articles.explainer:
• Representatives from India and Pakistan met for bilateral discussions regarding the implementation of the Indus Water Treaty (IWT).
• The talks focused on resolving technical disputes over hydropower projects in the Chenab basin and ensuring equitable water sharing.
• Both sides reiterated their commitment to upholding the provisions of the IWT, a crucial water-sharing agreement between the two nations.
• The Permanent Indus Commission, established under the treaty, facilitates such dialogues to address concerns and prevent conflicts.
• Despite broader political tensions, the IWT has largely endured, serving as a model for transboundary water cooperation.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests
prelimsPoints: India-Pakistan held talks on Indus Water Treaty (IWT) implementation.
Discussions focused on hydropower projects in Chenab basin.
Both nations committed to upholding IWT provisions.
Permanent Indus Commission facilitates such dialogues.
IWT is a crucial water-sharing agreement.
mainsPoints: Discuss the historical context and key provisions of the Indus Water Treaty (IWT) and its significance for India-Pakistan relations.
Analyze the challenges and disputes that arise in the implementation of transboundary water treaties, citing examples from the IWT.
Evaluate the role of the Permanent Indus Commission (PIC) in resolving technical disputes and fostering cooperation despite political tensions.
Examine the implications of hydropower projects on shared river basins and the need for equitable water resource management.
Critically assess the future of the IWT in the context of climate change, increasing water scarcity, and evolving geopolitical dynamics between India and Pakistan.
articles.imageDescription: A map showing the Indus River system and its tributaries.
articles.trivia: The Indus Water Treaty was brokered by the World Bank and signed in 1960, allocating the waters of the Indus River system between India and Pakistan.

articles.headline: Review Highlights Progress and Challenges in New Education Policy (NEP) Implementation
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Education released an interim review report on the implementation of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020.
• The report noted significant progress in foundational literacy and numeracy programs, curriculum reforms, and integration of vocational education.
• Challenges remain in infrastructure development, teacher training, and ensuring equitable access to digital learning resources, especially in rural areas.
• The NEP aims to transform India's education system to meet the demands of the 21st century, focusing on holistic, multidisciplinary learning.
• Stakeholder consultations are ongoing to address implementation bottlenecks and accelerate the policy's objectives.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Education, Human Resources
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors
prelimsPoints: Ministry of Education released interim review of NEP 2020 implementation.
Progress in foundational literacy, curriculum, vocational education.
Challenges include infrastructure, teacher training, digital access.
NEP aims for holistic, multidisciplinary learning.
Stakeholder consultations are ongoing.
mainsPoints: Discuss the key objectives and transformative potential of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020 in reforming India's education system.
Analyze the progress made in implementing foundational literacy and numeracy programs and integrating vocational education under NEP.
Evaluate the major challenges in achieving equitable access to quality education, particularly in terms of infrastructure and digital resources.
Examine the importance of teacher training and capacity building for the successful implementation of the NEP's pedagogical shifts.
Critically assess how the NEP addresses the demands of the 21st-century workforce and promotes holistic development among students.
articles.imageDescription: Students in a modern classroom setting with digital learning tools.
articles.trivia: The National Education Policy 2020 is the first education policy of the 21st century in India, replacing the thirty-four-year-old National Policy on Education (NPE), 1986.

articles.headline: India Joins Global Health Security Initiative, Pledges Enhanced Cooperation
articles.explainer:
• India formally joined the Global Health Security Initiative (GHSI), a forum dedicated to strengthening preparedness and response to global health threats.
• The move underscores India's commitment to international health cooperation and its growing role in global health governance.
• India pledged to share its expertise in vaccine manufacturing, digital health infrastructure, and epidemiological surveillance.
• GHSI aims to coordinate efforts among member states to prevent, detect, and respond to pandemics and other health emergencies.
• This membership is expected to enhance India's access to global health intelligence and best practices in public health management.
gsSubTopics: Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate
Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health
prelimsPoints: India joined the Global Health Security Initiative (GHSI).
GHSI strengthens preparedness for global health threats.
India pledged expertise in vaccine manufacturing, digital health, surveillance.
Aims to coordinate pandemic response.
Enhances India's access to global health intelligence.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of India's participation in global health security initiatives, particularly in the post-pandemic era.
Analyze India's contributions to global health, including its role as a 'pharmacy of the world' and its digital health advancements.
Evaluate the challenges and opportunities for international cooperation in strengthening preparedness and response to future pandemics.
Examine the concept of 'One Health' approach and its relevance in addressing emerging zoonotic diseases and environmental health threats.
Critically assess how India's engagement with GHSI can enhance its own public health infrastructure and epidemiological capabilities.
articles.imageDescription: A world map with interconnected health symbols.
articles.trivia: The COVID-19 pandemic highlighted the critical importance of global cooperation and robust health security frameworks.

highlights: Government introduces comprehensive National Digital Infrastructure Act 2025 for data governance, cybersecurity, and AI regulation.
RBI's Monetary Policy Committee maintains status quo on repo rate at 6.5% amidst persistent inflationary pressures.
India and France deepen strategic partnership in defence, space, and emerging technologies during annual dialogue.
Ministry of Environment launches expanded 'Project Dolphin' phase for enhanced conservation of Gangetic River Dolphins.
ISRO provides positive update on preparations for the Lunar Polar Exploration Mission (LUPEX) with JAXA, targeting 2026 launch.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to the National Digital Infrastructure Act (NDIA) 2025, consider the following statements:
1. It aims to consolidate existing laws on data governance, cybersecurity, and digital public infrastructure.
2. It proposes the establishment of a National Digital Authority (NDA) for oversight.
3. It mandates data localization for all sectors, including non-critical data.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct: The NDIA 2025 aims to consolidate existing laws on data governance, cybersecurity, and digital public infrastructure. Statement 2 is correct: It proposes the establishment of a National Digital Authority (NDA) for oversight. Statement 3 is incorrect: The Act proposes mandatory data localization for *critical sectors*, not necessarily all sectors, and the scope of 'critical' would be defined.

prelimsMocks.question: Which of the following bodies is responsible for setting the benchmark interest rate (repo rate) in India?
options: (b) Reserve Bank of India (RBI) Board of Directors
prelimsMocks.explanation: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) is a statutory body of the Reserve Bank of India, entrusted with the task of fixing the benchmark policy interest rate (repo rate) to achieve the inflation target.

prelimsMocks.question: The Lunar Polar Exploration Mission (LUPEX) is a collaborative venture between which two space agencies?
prelimsMocks.explanation: The Lunar Polar Exploration Mission (LUPEX) is a joint mission between the Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) and the Japan Aerospace Exploration Agency (JAXA).

prelimsMocks.question:
Consider the following statements regarding 'Project Dolphin' in India:
1. It focuses solely on the conservation of oceanic dolphins.
2. The Gangetic River Dolphin is India's National Aquatic Animal.
3. It emphasizes community engagement and habitat restoration.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is incorrect: Project Dolphin aims to conserve both riverine and oceanic dolphins. Statement 2 is correct: The Gangetic River Dolphin is indeed India's National Aquatic Animal. Statement 3 is correct: The project includes community engagement programs and habitat restoration efforts.

prelimsMocks.question:
Which of the following statements about the National Logistics Policy (NLP) is/are correct?
1. It aims to reduce logistics costs as a percentage of GDP to global benchmarks.
2. The Unified Logistics Interface Platform (ULIP) is a key initiative under NLP.
3. It focuses exclusively on road transport infrastructure.
Select the correct answer using the code given below:
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct: NLP aims to reduce logistics costs to global benchmarks. Statement 2 is correct: ULIP is a key digital initiative under NLP. Statement 3 is incorrect: NLP focuses on multi-modal logistics, including roads, railways, waterways, and air, not exclusively on road transport.

mainsMocks.question: The proposed National Digital Infrastructure Act (NDIA) 2025 seeks to create a unified regulatory framework for India's digital economy. Discuss the necessity of such a comprehensive law in the context of emerging technologies and evolving cyber threats. What are the potential challenges in its implementation?
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors; GS3: Cybersecurity, IT
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce NDIA 2025 and its broad objectives. Explain the necessity by discussing the fragmented nature of existing laws, rapid technological advancements (AI, IoT), data privacy concerns, cybersecurity threats, and the need for a robust digital economy. Detail potential challenges such as balancing innovation with regulation, ensuring effective enforcement, addressing data localization implications, and achieving stakeholder consensus.

mainsMocks.question: India's indigenous defence production has been a key component of its 'Aatmanirbhar Bharat' vision. Analyze the strategic implications of boosting domestic defence manufacturing for India's national security and its role in regional geopolitics. What policy measures are critical for sustaining this growth?
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS3: Security challenges and their management in border areas; Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
mainsMocks.approach: Define 'Aatmanirbhar Bharat' in defence. Explain strategic implications: reduced import dependency, enhanced technological sovereignty, stronger deterrent capabilities, potential for defence exports, and job creation. Discuss its role in regional geopolitics, projecting India as a responsible power. Policy measures should include R&D funding, streamlined procurement, private sector involvement (MSMEs, startups), foreign collaboration, and skill development.

mainsMocks.question: The expansion of 'Project Dolphin' signifies India's commitment to aquatic biodiversity conservation. Discuss the ecological significance of the Gangetic River Dolphin as an indicator species for river health. What are the primary threats to its survival, and how can integrated conservation strategies, including community participation, address these challenges?
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS3: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment, Biodiversity
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce Project Dolphin and the Gangetic River Dolphin's status as National Aquatic Animal. Explain its ecological significance as an indicator species for the health of the Ganga-Brahmaputra river system. Detail primary threats: pollution, habitat degradation, illegal fishing, dams. Discuss integrated strategies: habitat restoration, anti-poaching measures, pollution control, and critically, the role of local communities (awareness, sustainable livelihoods) in ensuring long-term conservation success.

quickBytes.text: The National Digital Authority (NDA) is proposed under the NDIA 2025 for digital governance oversight.
Repo rate is the rate at which the Reserve Bank of India lends money to commercial banks.
The term 'Indo-Pacific' emphasizes the interconnectedness of the Indian and Pacific Oceans.
The Gangetic River Dolphin is listed as 'Endangered' on the IUCN Red List.
LUPEX (Lunar Polar Exploration Mission) is a joint mission of ISRO (India) and JAXA (Japan).
ULIP (Unified Logistics Interface Platform) is a digital initiative under the National Logistics Policy.
Electoral Bonds were introduced in India through the Finance Act, 2017.
ABHA (Ayushman Bharat Health Account) is a unique health ID under Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission.
CBDR-RC (Common but Differentiated Responsibilities and Respective Capabilities) is a climate justice principle.
The Border Roads Organisation (BRO) constructs and maintains roads in India's border areas.

GS1.headline: Government Unveils 'Smart Cities 2.0' Vision for Sustainable Urban Development
GS1.explainer:
• The Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs launched the 'Smart Cities 2.0' initiative, building upon the successes and lessons learned from the first phase.
• The new vision emphasizes a holistic approach to urban planning, integrating climate resilience, digital governance, and citizen participation.
• Focus areas include sustainable mobility, green infrastructure, waste-to-wealth initiatives, and affordable housing solutions.
• The initiative aims to create 'liveable cities' that are economically vibrant, environmentally sustainable, and socially inclusive.
• Advanced technologies, including AI and IoT, will be leveraged for efficient urban management and public service delivery.
gsSubTopics: Urbanization, their problems and their remedies
Salient features of Indian Society
Role of women and women’s organization, population and associated issues, poverty and developmental issues, urbanization, their problems and their remedies
prelimsPoints: Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs launched 'Smart Cities 2.0'.
Emphasizes climate resilience, digital governance, citizen participation.
Focus areas: sustainable mobility, green infrastructure, waste-to-wealth.
Aims to create 'liveable cities'.
Leverages AI and IoT for urban management.
mainsPoints: Discuss the evolution of India's Smart Cities Mission and the necessity for a 'Smart Cities 2.0' vision in addressing contemporary urban challenges.
Analyze the concept of 'liveable cities' and how the new initiative aims to integrate economic vibrancy, environmental sustainability, and social inclusivity.
Evaluate the role of digital governance and advanced technologies (AI, IoT) in enhancing urban management and public service delivery.
Examine the challenges in implementing sustainable urban planning strategies, particularly in rapidly urbanizing Indian cities.
Critically assess how 'Smart Cities 2.0' can contribute to climate resilience and address issues of urban poverty and inequality.
GS1.imageDescription: A futuristic city skyline with green spaces and smart infrastructure.
GS1.trivia: The Smart Cities Mission was launched by Prime Minister Narendra Modi in 2015, with the objective of promoting cities that provide core infrastructure and give a decent quality of life to its citizens.

GS2.headline: Government Unveils Comprehensive National Digital Infrastructure Act 2025
GS2.explainer:
• The Union Cabinet approved the draft National Digital Infrastructure Act (NDIA) 2025, aiming to consolidate existing laws on data governance, cybersecurity, and digital public infrastructure.
• The Act proposes a unified regulatory framework for digital services, focusing on data privacy, secure digital transactions, and responsible AI development.
• Key provisions include mandatory data localization for critical sectors, establishment of a National Digital Authority (NDA) for oversight, and penalties for data breaches.
• It seeks to balance innovation with user protection, addressing concerns around digital monopolies and ensuring equitable access to digital services.
• NDIA 2025 is expected to replace fragmented laws and provide a robust legal foundation for India's growing digital economy.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors
Information and Communication Technologies
Cybersecurity
Data Protection
E-governance
prelimsPoints: NDIA 2025 aims to consolidate laws on data governance, cybersecurity, and DPI.
It proposes the establishment of a National Digital Authority (NDA).
Key aspects include data localization and penalties for data breaches.
The Act seeks to replace fragmented existing digital laws.
mainsPoints: Discuss the necessity of a unified digital infrastructure act in the context of India's digital growth and evolving cyber threats.
Analyze the potential impact of data localization mandates on foreign tech companies and India's digital economy.
Evaluate the role of the proposed National Digital Authority (NDA) in ensuring regulatory coherence and effective enforcement.
Critically examine how the Act balances innovation, user protection, and national security concerns in the digital space.
Elaborate on the challenges and opportunities in implementing a comprehensive digital governance framework like NDIA 2025.
GS2.imageDescription: Illustration of digital infrastructure with data streams and security symbols.
GS2.trivia: India is one of the fastest-growing digital economies, with over 800 million internet users as of early 2020s.

GS2.headline: India and France Deepen Strategic Partnership in Annual Dialogue
GS2.explainer:
• The annual India-France Strategic Dialogue concluded in New Delhi, focusing on enhanced cooperation in defence, space, and emerging technologies.
• Both nations reaffirmed their commitment to a free, open, and inclusive Indo-Pacific, agreeing to coordinate efforts on maritime security and regional stability.
• Discussions included joint defence manufacturing initiatives, transfer of critical technologies, and collaboration on sustainable development goals.
• India's National Security Advisor and the French President's Diplomatic Advisor led the talks, underscoring the high-level engagement.
• The dialogue is a cornerstone of the India-France strategic partnership, which has gained momentum in recent years.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests
prelimsPoints: Annual India-France Strategic Dialogue held in New Delhi.
Focus areas include defence, space, and emerging technologies.
Both nations reiterated commitment to a free Indo-Pacific.
Discussions covered joint manufacturing and technology transfer.
mainsPoints: Analyze the significance of the India-France strategic partnership in the evolving geopolitical landscape of the Indo-Pacific.
Discuss the role of defence and space cooperation in strengthening India's strategic autonomy and technological capabilities.
Evaluate how India and France can collaborate to address global challenges such as climate change and sustainable development.
Examine the implications of enhanced bilateral ties on India's 'Make in India' initiative, particularly in the defence sector.
Critically assess the convergences and divergences in India and France's approach to multilateralism and global governance.
GS2.imageDescription: Flags of India and France waving together.
GS2.trivia: France was the first P5 nation to establish a strategic partnership with India in 1998.

GS2.headline: Supreme Court Bench to Review Electoral Bonds Transparency Petitions
GS2.explainer:
• A special bench of the Supreme Court of India is set to hear a series of petitions challenging the transparency and constitutionality of the Electoral Bonds scheme.
• Petitioners argue that the scheme promotes anonymous funding to political parties, undermining electoral transparency and the right to information.
• The government has defended the scheme as a measure to curb black money in political funding, ensuring donor anonymity.
• The court's decision is anticipated to have significant implications for electoral funding reforms and political accountability in India.
• Previous hearings have focused on the balance between donor privacy and public's right to know the source of political funds.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure
Separation of powers between various organs dispute redressal mechanisms and institutions
Salient features of the Representation of People’s Act
prelimsPoints: Supreme Court bench to review petitions on Electoral Bonds scheme.
Petitions challenge transparency and constitutionality of the scheme.
Government defends the scheme as a curb on black money.
Scheme allows anonymous funding to political parties.
mainsPoints: Discuss the constitutional validity and ethical concerns surrounding the Electoral Bonds scheme in India.
Analyze how the Electoral Bonds scheme impacts electoral transparency and the public's right to know about political funding.
Evaluate the arguments for and against donor anonymity in political financing, considering its implications for democratic processes.
Examine the role of the judiciary in ensuring electoral reforms and upholding the principles of free and fair elections.
Suggest alternative mechanisms for political funding that can enhance transparency while addressing the issue of black money in elections.
GS2.imageDescription: Supreme Court of India building facade.
GS2.trivia: Electoral bonds were introduced in India through the Finance Act, 2017, and later amendments to the Reserve Bank of India Act, 1934, and the Representation of the People Act, 1951.

GS2.headline: Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission Crosses Major Milestones in Digital Health Adoption
GS2.explainer:
• The National Health Authority (NHA) announced that the Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM) has achieved significant milestones, including over 500 million Ayushman Bharat Health Accounts (ABHA).
• The mission is rapidly expanding its network of healthcare providers and digital health records, enhancing accessibility and efficiency.
• Integration of private hospitals and clinics into the ABDM ecosystem is progressing, facilitating seamless sharing of patient data with consent.
• The digital health ecosystem aims to empower individuals with control over their health records and improve healthcare delivery across the nation.
• Future plans include leveraging AI and machine learning for predictive analytics in public health.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources
E-governance- applications, models, successes, limitations, and potential
prelimsPoints: ABDM achieved over 500 million Ayushman Bharat Health Accounts (ABHA).
NHA is the implementing agency for ABDM.
Mission aims to create a digital health ecosystem.
Focus on integrating private healthcare providers.
Future plans include AI for predictive analytics in health.
mainsPoints: Discuss the transformative potential of the Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission in revolutionizing healthcare delivery and access in India.
Analyze the challenges involved in ensuring data privacy and security within a vast digital health ecosystem like ABDM.
Evaluate the role of ABHA in empowering individuals and promoting patient-centric healthcare.
Examine how ABDM can bridge the healthcare divide between urban and rural areas and improve health outcomes.
Critically assess the implications of integrating AI and machine learning into public health initiatives under ABDM for disease surveillance and prevention.
GS2.imageDescription: A graphic depicting interconnected digital health records and patient profiles.
GS2.trivia: The Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission was launched nationwide in September 2021, building on a pilot project.

GS2.headline: India Prepares for Crucial Role in Upcoming UN Climate Summit (COP30)
GS2.explainer:
• India's Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change outlined its priorities and negotiation strategies for the upcoming UN Climate Summit (COP30 in Brazil).
• India will advocate for equitable climate action, emphasizing the principle of Common but Differentiated Responsibilities and Respective Capabilities (CBDR-RC).
• Focus areas include securing enhanced climate finance for developing nations, technology transfer for green solutions, and adaptation measures.
• India plans to highlight its domestic achievements in renewable energy expansion and climate resilience, urging developed nations to meet their commitments.
• The summit is expected to finalize mechanisms for global carbon markets and review progress on the Paris Agreement goals.
gsSubTopics: Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests
Environmental pollution and degradation
keyTerms: Common but Differentiated Responsibilities and Respective Capabilities (CBDR-RC)
prelimsPoints: India outlined priorities for COP30 in Brazil.
Advocating for CBDR-RC principle.
Key demands: enhanced climate finance and technology transfer.
India will highlight its renewable energy achievements.
COP30 to finalize global carbon market mechanisms.
mainsPoints: Discuss India's historical stance on climate change negotiations and its evolving role in global climate diplomacy.
Analyze the significance of the CBDR-RC principle for developing nations like India in achieving climate justice.
Evaluate the challenges in securing adequate climate finance and technology transfer from developed to developing countries.
Examine India's domestic climate action initiatives, particularly in renewable energy, and their contribution to its international commitments.
Critically assess the potential outcomes of COP30 regarding global carbon markets and the review of Paris Agreement goals.
GS2.imageDescription: Globe with climate change symbols and national flags.
GS2.trivia: The first Conference of Parties (COP) to the UNFCCC was held in Berlin, Germany, in 1995.

GS2.headline: Skill India Mission 3.0 Launched with Focus on Future Skills and Green Jobs
GS2.explainer:
• The Ministry of Skill Development and Entrepreneurship launched Skill India Mission 3.0, an upgraded version of its flagship skilling program.
• The new phase emphasizes upskilling and reskilling in emerging technologies like AI, IoT, and data science, aligned with industry 4.0 demands.
• A significant focus is placed on promoting 'green jobs' and sustainable livelihoods, reflecting India's commitment to a green economy.
• The mission aims to integrate formal education with vocational training, ensuring greater employability and entrepreneurship opportunities.
• Public-private partnerships and international collaborations are being strengthened to provide world-class skill development infrastructure.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Human Resources
Skill development and employment
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors
prelimsPoints: Skill India Mission 3.0 launched by Ministry of Skill Development.
Focuses on emerging technologies (AI, IoT) and green jobs.
Aims to integrate formal education with vocational training.
Strengthening public-private partnerships and international collaborations.
Targets industry 4.0 demands.
mainsPoints: Discuss the evolution of Skill India Mission and the necessity for its 3.0 iteration in the context of changing global economic and technological landscapes.
Analyze the potential of 'green jobs' in driving sustainable development and creating new employment opportunities in India.
Evaluate the challenges in bridging the skill gap in emerging technologies and ensuring that India's workforce is future-ready.
Examine the role of public-private partnerships and international collaborations in enhancing the quality and reach of skill development programs.
Critically assess how Skill India Mission 3.0 can contribute to India's demographic dividend and foster an entrepreneurial ecosystem.
GS2.imageDescription: Young people engaged in skill training using modern technology.
GS2.trivia: The National Skill Development Mission, or Skill India Mission, was launched in 2015 to create convergence across sectors and states in terms of skill training activities.

GS2.headline: India-Pakistan Hold Bilateral Talks on Indus Water Treaty Implementation
GS2.explainer:
• Representatives from India and Pakistan met for bilateral discussions regarding the implementation of the Indus Water Treaty (IWT).
• The talks focused on resolving technical disputes over hydropower projects in the Chenab basin and ensuring equitable water sharing.
• Both sides reiterated their commitment to upholding the provisions of the IWT, a crucial water-sharing agreement between the two nations.
• The Permanent Indus Commission, established under the treaty, facilitates such dialogues to address concerns and prevent conflicts.
• Despite broader political tensions, the IWT has largely endured, serving as a model for transboundary water cooperation.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests
prelimsPoints: India-Pakistan held talks on Indus Water Treaty (IWT) implementation.
Discussions focused on hydropower projects in Chenab basin.
Both nations committed to upholding IWT provisions.
Permanent Indus Commission facilitates such dialogues.
IWT is a crucial water-sharing agreement.
mainsPoints: Discuss the historical context and key provisions of the Indus Water Treaty (IWT) and its significance for India-Pakistan relations.
Analyze the challenges and disputes that arise in the implementation of transboundary water treaties, citing examples from the IWT.
Evaluate the role of the Permanent Indus Commission (PIC) in resolving technical disputes and fostering cooperation despite political tensions.
Examine the implications of hydropower projects on shared river basins and the need for equitable water resource management.
Critically assess the future of the IWT in the context of climate change, increasing water scarcity, and evolving geopolitical dynamics between India and Pakistan.
GS2.imageDescription: A map showing the Indus River system and its tributaries.
GS2.trivia: The Indus Water Treaty was brokered by the World Bank and signed in 1960, allocating the waters of the Indus River system between India and Pakistan.

GS2.headline: Review Highlights Progress and Challenges in New Education Policy (NEP) Implementation
GS2.explainer:
• The Ministry of Education released an interim review report on the implementation of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020.
• The report noted significant progress in foundational literacy and numeracy programs, curriculum reforms, and integration of vocational education.
• Challenges remain in infrastructure development, teacher training, and ensuring equitable access to digital learning resources, especially in rural areas.
• The NEP aims to transform India's education system to meet the demands of the 21st century, focusing on holistic, multidisciplinary learning.
• Stakeholder consultations are ongoing to address implementation bottlenecks and accelerate the policy's objectives.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Education, Human Resources
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors

Progress in foundational literacy, curriculum, vocational education.
Challenges include infrastructure, teacher training, digital access.
NEP aims for holistic, multidisciplinary learning.
Stakeholder consultations are ongoing.
mainsPoints: Discuss the key objectives and transformative potential of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020 in reforming India's education system.
Analyze the progress made in implementing foundational literacy and numeracy programs and integrating vocational education under NEP.
Evaluate the major challenges in achieving equitable access to quality education, particularly in terms of infrastructure and digital resources.
Examine the importance of teacher training and capacity building for the successful implementation of the NEP's pedagogical shifts.
Critically assess how the NEP addresses the demands of the 21st-century workforce and promotes holistic development among students.
GS2.imageDescription: Students in a modern classroom setting with digital learning tools.
GS2.trivia: The National Education Policy 2020 is the first education policy of the 21st century in India, replacing the thirty-four-year-old National Policy on Education (NPE), 1986.

GS2.headline: India Joins Global Health Security Initiative, Pledges Enhanced Cooperation
GS2.explainer:
• India formally joined the Global Health Security Initiative (GHSI), a forum dedicated to strengthening preparedness and response to global health threats.
• The move underscores India's commitment to international health cooperation and its growing role in global health governance.
• India pledged to share its expertise in vaccine manufacturing, digital health infrastructure, and epidemiological surveillance.
• GHSI aims to coordinate efforts among member states to prevent, detect, and respond to pandemics and other health emergencies.
• This membership is expected to enhance India's access to global health intelligence and best practices in public health management.

mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of India's participation in global health security initiatives, particularly in the post-pandemic era.
Analyze India's contributions to global health, including its role as a 'pharmacy of the world' and its digital health advancements.
Evaluate the challenges and opportunities for international cooperation in strengthening preparedness and response to future pandemics.
Examine the concept of 'One Health' approach and its relevance in addressing emerging zoonotic diseases and environmental health threats.
